subject: phux-emdv
message: flaky test: eof_detach::pty_eof_drives_terminal_closed_to_attached_client fails under full-parallel nextest

bead.title: flaky test: eof_detach::pty_eof_drives_terminal_closed_to_attached_client fails under full-parallel nextest
bead.description:
OBSERVED: phux-server::eof_detach pty_eof_drives_terminal_closed_to_attached_client intermittently FAILS during 'cargo nextest run --workspace' (full ~1380-test parallel run, observed twice this cycle) but PASSES reliably in isolation (5/5 across separate runs). Classic timing flake under CPU contention: the PTY EOF -> TERMINAL_CLOSED -> attached-client notification has a race/timeout that only loses when the machine is saturated by 200+ concurrent tests. On the 2-core CI runner this will intermittently red the suite.

LIKELY CAUSE: a fixed sleep/timeout or an ordering assumption between the PTY reader thread seeing EOF, the actor emitting TERMINAL_CLOSED, and the test draining the client mailbox. FIX DIRECTION: replace any fixed-duration wait with a poll-until-condition (bounded retry), or raise the timeout generously, or make the test await the specific frame rather than a deadline. Find the test in crates/phux-server/tests/ (eof_detach integration) and the EOF->close path in terminal_actor. SEVERITY: medium (CI flake, not a product bug) — but it will waste CI cycles and erode trust in green.
bead.notes: THIRD flake observed 2026-06-07: phux::bin/phux commands::run::tests::run_nonce_is_unique_across_invocations fails under full-parallel nextest, passes 3/3 isolated. NOTE: different mechanism from the PTY-timing flakes (eof_detach, route_input_no_resize) — this is a NONCE-COLLISION: run_nonce likely derives from a coarse clock (seconds/millis) or a non-atomic counter, so two invocations within the same tick collide when the suite runs them back-to-back. Real (if minor) robustness bug, not just a test-harness issue: the run nonce should use a monotonic atomic counter or higher-resolution+random source so production 'phux run' calls in tight succession can't collide either. Worth a small dedicated fix separate from the timing-flake test-grouping.

subject: phux-ahk.1
message: audit: terminal_inputs per-pane log grows unbounded (write-only in production)

bead.title: audit: terminal_inputs per-pane log grows unbounded (write-only in production)
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk, verified by skeptic + maintainer read). ServerState.terminal_inputs (HashMap<TerminalId, Vec<TerminalInput>>, crates/phux-server/src/state/registry.rs:31, appended in record_terminal_input ~765) accumulates every keystroke/mouse/focus/paste recorded against a pane and is only cleared on reap (forget_terminal_bookkeeping -> remove, ~708). For a long-lived pane it grows without bound.

Aggravating: in SHIPPED code the log has NO production reader. The only reader is the #[cfg(test)] terminal_input_log_for (~880). The state/mod.rs module doc claims 'the PTY writer task reads from this log (phux-byc.4)', but that path never landed — keystroke->PTY writes happen via a different path, so the log is effectively append-only/inspection-only in production.

DECISION NEEDED (why this is a bead not a blind fix): either (a) cap it (ring-buffer / VecDeque, keep last N, or TTL-evict) preserving the multi-client merge-point semantics the doc describes, or (b) if byc.4's PTY-writer consumer is dead, feature-gate or delete the log entirely. (a) bounds memory cheaply; (b) removes dead state. Pick one with the byc.4 owner. Tests push 3 entries so any cap >= a few thousand keeps them green.

SEVERITY: medium (slow leak, only for very long-lived high-input panes).
bead.notes: FIXED on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity (unmerged), commit 4a70200. Per audit reframe (it was never WRITTEN in prod, not just unread -> no leak), DELETED the dead scaffolding: terminal_inputs field, record_terminal_input, terminal_input_log_for, the forget_terminal_bookkeeping cleanup, and the test of the dead log. Corrected the false module doc. TerminalInput type kept (live mailbox event). Maintainer decision was delete, not bound.

subject: phux-ahk.2
message: audit: per-consumer snapshot+cursor/mode recomputed every tick (scales with consumer count)

bead.title: audit: per-consumer snapshot+cursor/mode recomputed every tick (scales with consumer count)
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk). The actor holds ONE shared SnapshotSynthesizer (terminal_actor/mod.rs:88 synth: RefCell<...>), but synthesize_against_reference is called once PER CONSUMER PER TICK (each consumer has its own ConsumerReference). Inside it, two terminal-only (consumer-independent) computations are redone N times for N consumers on a shared pane:
1. self.render_state.update(terminal) (synthesizer.rs:654) — re-snapshots the whole terminal per consumer.
2. ReferenceCursorMode::capture(&snapshot, terminal) (synthesizer.rs:718) — 5x terminal.mode() FFI calls + cursor queries (reference.rs:95-99), per consumer.
Both depend only on the shared terminal state within a tick, so with N consumers it is 5N mode-FFI calls and N full snapshots where 5 and 1 would do.

FIX SKETCH: hoist the per-tick terminal snapshot + cursor/mode capture to once per tick in tick_emit, compute the shared bits once, and pass them into each consumer's diff (synthesize_against_reference takes the precomputed snapshot/live_cm instead of recomputing). Care: RefCell borrow of synth, snapshot lifetime, and consumers attaching mid-tick. The auditor flagged only the mode FFI; the render_state.update redundancy is the bigger cost.

SEVERITY: medium (idle/steady-state CPU on multi-consumer panes; harmless for single-consumer).
bead.notes:
RISK REFINEMENT (for the implementer): the hoist is semantically identical, not just an optimization — tick_emit runs synchronously on the actor thread and the Terminal is NOT mutated during the per-consumer loop, so every consumer already observes the same terminal state this tick. Capturing render_state.update(terminal) + ReferenceCursorMode::capture ONCE before the loop and reusing across consumers cannot change output. Shape: split synthesize_against_reference(&mut self, terminal, reference) into (a) a once-per-tick snapshot+cursor/mode capture borrowing self.render_state mut, then (b) a per-consumer diff_against_reference(&self, &snapshot, &live_cm, &mut reference). Watch the borrow split (update is &mut self.render_state once; the diffs are & + &mut per-reference) and keep reset_geometry driven off the shared snapshot dims. Existing multi-consumer tick tests (tick_emit_serves_every_consumer_on_a_shared_pane, tick_emit_mixed_mode_*) guard rendering parity. Low correctness risk, moderate mechanical effort; deserves its own focused PR.
AUDIT REFRAME + partial fix on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity. KEY FINDING: the per-consumer tick/SnapshotSynthesizer path is DORMANT in production — the shipping client never negotiates OutputMode::StateSync, so tick_emit early-returns (no consumer wants state sync) and synthesize_against_reference's per-consumer recompute never runs for a human attach. So ahk.2's 5N-mode-FFI/N-snapshot cost is NOT the felt lag. The CONCRETE attach cost it/ahk.4 pointed at — register_consumer priming a full per-consumer reference + cursor/mode capture on EVERY attach — IS real and is FIXED (commit c345b49): both gated behind tick_managed, so a Raw attach is O(1) instead of O(rows*cols). The StateSync-path hoist (compute render_state.update + ReferenceCursorMode::capture once/tick) remains valid future work for WHEN StateSync ships, but is not worth doing against dead code now.

subject: phux-ahk.4
message: audit: prime_reference allocates a fresh Vec per row at attach (reuse reset_geometry buffers)

bead.title: audit: prime_reference allocates a fresh Vec per row at attach (reuse reset_geometry buffers)
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk). SnapshotSynthesizer::prime_reference allocates a fresh Vec<u8> per viewport row at consumer registration (crates/phux-server/src/grid/synthesizer.rs:803) — 24 allocations for an 80x24 grid per attach. reset_geometry (grid/reference.rs:47) already pre-sizes rows_body to empty Vecs; prime could populate those in place by swap (same pattern as synthesize_against_reference ~707) instead of allocating fresh.

Not on the hot tick path (attach-time only), so low priority. FIX SKETCH: iterate the preallocated empty row bodies and fill in place via a single scratch buffer + swap. SEVERITY: low.
bead.notes: SUPERSEDED by commit c345b49 (branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity). Rather than pool the per-row Vec allocs in prime_reference, register_consumer now SKIPS prime_reference entirely for raw (human) consumers — the reference it builds is never read on that path. So the per-row alloc cost is gone in the dominant mode without the pooling rework. The pooling optimization still applies if/when a tick-managed (StateSync) consumer attaches frequently, but is low priority.

subject: phux-ahk.3
message: audit: WebSocket write_frame allocates a Vec per frame via to_vec()

bead.title: audit: WebSocket write_frame allocates a Vec per frame via to_vec()
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk). WsWriter::write_frame (crates/phux-server/src/transport.rs:216) calls frame.to_vec() to own the borrowed frame slice for Message::Binary. Every TERMINAL_OUTPUT frame to a WebSocket client (phux-web) allocates+copies the full frame, per-consumer per-tick on the WS path. The UDS path (UdsWriter::write_frame ~91) writes the borrowed slice directly with no copy.

FIX SKETCH: check whether tokio-tungstenite's Message::Binary can take Bytes/Arc<[u8]> to avoid the copy; if not, pool/reuse a frame buffer at the WsWriter level. Needs a small library-capability investigation (hence a bead). SEVERITY: low (only the WS transport; UDS unaffected).
bead.notes: Still open. Confirmed by audit (WsWriter::write_frame copies the encoded frame per send). Investigated during branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity work: tokio-tungstenite/tungstenite pinned at 0.24 in Cargo.lock, whose Message::Binary takes Vec<u8>; the Bytes-payload API landed in tungstenite 0.26. The caller also hands write_frame a &[u8] borrowed from a reused encode buffer, so a copy is needed to own it for the async send regardless. UNBLOCK: bump tungstenite to >=0.26 then switch to Message::Binary(Bytes). WS path is the browser consumer, not the UDS/TUI path.

subject: phux-ahk
message: Edge-case + scroll + memory/efficiency hardening pass

bead.title: Edge-case + scroll + memory/efficiency hardening pass
bead.description: Audit and add coverage: (1) scrollback/scrolling correctness under resize, wide glyphs, huge output, alt-screen toggles; (2) memory: scrollback mirror growth bounds, per-consumer reference grid footprint, predictive-echo buffers; (3) efficiency: hot-path allocations on the tick/emit path, diff cost. Add edge-case tests (resize storms, rapid attach/detach, multi-consumer). Identify and file concrete sub-issues for any real problems found rather than fixing blindly.
bead.notes:
AUDIT PASS 2026-06-07 (multi-agent, adversarially verified): ran 3 parallel auditors (scroll/resize correctness, memory bounds, hot-path efficiency) + per-finding skeptic verification (default-to-refuted). 6 findings, all survived verification. FIXED + landed (PR #80, merged): (1) emit_instants RTT map unbounded growth for never-acking consumers -> MAX_EMIT_INSTANTS=256 oldest-evicted cap + regression test; (2) format!-alloc in scrollback SU synth -> write! inline. FILED as children for design-decision/lower-priority remediation: phux-ahk.1 (terminal_inputs per-pane log unbounded, write-only in prod), phux-ahk.2 (per-consumer snapshot+cursor/mode recomputed every tick, scales with consumer count), phux-ahk.3 (WS write_frame per-frame Vec), phux-ahk.4 (prime_reference per-row alloc at attach). Audit mandate (identify + file sub-issues) fulfilled; parent stays open until ahk.1-4 remediation lands.
AUDIT 2026-06-08 (adversarial, 17 findings all verified) — NEW high-value color-fidelity bugs found beyond the filed children, FIXED on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity (commit 607dd22): (1) underline + overline were never emitted by the server snapshot synth OR the client pane renderer -> all neovim undercurls/cursorline + p10k underlines flattened; (2) the server's emit_sgr_delta gate keyed only on attribute flags (styles_equal ignored fg/bg), so adjacent same-attribute cells differing only in COLOR dropped the second color (multi-color ls / syntax highlighting reverted to the prior run's color on the synthesized snapshot); (3) emit_cell pushed the blank-cell space BEFORE reading style, dropping the background of colored-but-blank regions. All three surfaced only after attach/re-attach/resize (steady-state is raw byte-faithful PTV) — matching the maintainer's 'color glitch on attach'. Fixed via a shared phux-protocol::sgr encoder (single source of truth, prevents re-drift) + per-cell full-pen (Style+fg+bg) tracking + emit-before-blank reorder, with round-trip color tests. See branch for the lag work too (ahk.2/ahk.4 reframe, chunk coalescing, Bytes forward).

subject: phux-e3mo
message: Snapshot fidelity follow-ups: post-resync SGR continuity (#9) + epilogue mode gaps

bead.title: Snapshot fidelity follow-ups: post-resync SGR continuity (#9) + epilogue mode gaps
bead.description: Deferred from the 2026-06-08 ahk audit (lower confidence / lower value than the landed color fixes on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity). (A) #9 POST-SNAPSHOT SGR CONTINUITY: the snapshot epilogue resets SGR to 0 and the live broadcast pump then resumes raw relative PTV deltas, with no guarantee the client mirror's pen matches the server's at the snapshot boundary. Plausible narrow-window color glitch right after attach with p10k (self-heals on next prompt redraw). LOWEST-confidence survivor; the epilogue contract is SHARED with the StateSync path. DO NOT change it without a real p10k capture proving the mechanism. (B) EPILOGUE MODE GAPS: emit_epilogue restores no DECAWM(7)/DECSTBM scroll-region/DECOM origin mode, and a cursor-not-in-viewport snapshot homes the cursor to ESC[H (visible top-left flash) instead of hiding/omitting it. Both self-heal on the next PTV frame; only worth doing if already reworking emit_epilogue for (A), else document that snapshot fidelity is grid-only by design. Files: crates/phux-server/src/grid/synthesizer.rs emit_epilogue / the pump resume in runtime/attach.rs.

subject: phux-5vsr
message: Authenticated, encrypted transport for remote consumers (no SSH tunnel)

bead.title: Authenticated, encrypted transport for remote consumers (no SSH tunnel)
bead.description:
Driver: phux-mobile (native iOS consumer) needs a phone to connect to a user's phux server directly over a network. Today that path doesn't exist securely.

Current state:
- The WebSocket transport exists (crates/phux-server/src/transport.rs: TCP + RFC 6455, one frame per binary message, PHUX_WS_ADDR) but is PLAINTEXT and UNAUTHENTICATED — fine for the local/trusted browser client, unsafe to expose to a remote phone.
- The only secure remote path today is SSH-tunneled 'phux server --stdio' (docs/operations.md 'Federation trust model'): SSH provides auth+encryption. A mobile app can't reasonably carry an SSH client UX.
- QUIC + mutual TLS is the documented v0.2+ future (ADR-0007) and is tracked as phux-84yt (P0, satellite-framed) under the federation epic phux-klxy.

Scope of THIS bead (nearer-term, mobile-driven; distinct from full federation):
- Decide an auth + encryption scheme for a remote consumer connecting to a single phux server without SSH and without the full hub-and-spoke federation. Candidates: TLS + bearer/pairing token over the existing WS transport; or mutual TLS; or pairing-code flow. Needs an ADR in phux/ADR (it closes a security design space).
- Add it to the WebSocket Transport path (PeerIdentity already exists in phux_protocol::policy) so an authenticated, encrypted remote consumer is a first-class peer.
- Confirm OutputMode::StateSync (ADR-0018) is the intended mode for a high-latency/lossy mobile link vs. Raw (currently the default); document the recommendation for remote consumers.

Relationship: this may be folded into / superseded by the QUIC work (phux-84yt) if QUIC lands first; file as the WS-auth path so the team can choose sequencing. Keep it 'public phux framework work' per AGENTS.md.

Worktree prepared for this: /Users/phall/workspace/phux-net (branch feat/remote-consumer-transport, off main).
bead.design: No homegrown crypto (CONTRIBUTING.md): lean on TLS (rustls) + a token, or reuse SSH's envelope; do not invent a handshake. The wire codec is transport-agnostic already (transport.rs seam), so auth/encryption is a transport-layer concern, not a protocol-layer one — avoid adding auth frames to the wire spec unless an ADR justifies it.
bead.acceptance_criteria: An ADR records the chosen remote-consumer auth+encryption approach and its trust boundary; the WebSocket transport can require it; a remote consumer (e.g. phux-mobile) can attach over a network with auth+encryption and no SSH tunnel; OutputMode guidance for remote consumers is documented.

Reachability linchpin tracked separately (relay bead). Write this up as an ADR on worktree feat/remote-consumer-transport.

subject: phux-yx6y
message: Relay/rendezvous so a phone can reach a NAT'd phux server

bead.title: Relay/rendezvous so a phone can reach a NAT'd phux server
bead.description:
The linchpin for 'connect from anywhere'. A phux server runs behind NAT/CGNAT; a remote phone (phux-mobile) cannot initiate an inbound connection to it. Solution: a relay with a public IP that BOTH the server and the client connect to OUTBOUND (NAT permits outbound), and which bridges them — like Cloudflare Tunnel / Tailscale DERP / ngrok / VS Code tunnels.

Server side (phux): the server can dial OUT to a configured relay and accept consumers routed back through it, over the wss transport (phux-5vsr). Client side: phux-mobile connects to the relay, authenticates, and is bridged to the user's server.

Notes:
- Easiest over TCP+TLS/:443 (commodity L7 proxying); this is a key reason wss is Phase 1 (see phux-5vsr).
- The hosted relay is also the natural subscription boundary for phux-mobile (local-network free, remote+push paid) — product/ops side belongs to phux-mobile.
- Alternatives to consider in the ADR: STUN/ICE hole-punching (fails on symmetric NAT -> needs TURN anyway), or requiring an overlay VPN (Tailscale) for power users.
Relates: phux-5vsr (wss auth), phux-84yt (QUIC), phux-mobile-ax5.

subject: phux-0wmf
message: WebTransport (HTTP/3 over QUIC) for the browser client

bead.title: WebTransport (HTTP/3 over QUIC) for the browser client
bead.description:
The browser-flavored version of the QUIC upgrade (phux-84yt). Browsers can't open raw QUIC/UDP sockets; WebTransport (over HTTP/3 = QUIC) is their only door to QUIC-class transport (multiplexed streams, no HOL blocking, connection migration, datagrams).

Scope: when phux wants the QUIC experience in clients/phux-web, add a WebTransport path on the server (quinn + h3 + webtransport) sharing the same QUIC server as native QUIC. NOTE: this is for phux-web only — phux-mobile is native and uses raw QUIC via quinn in its FFI bridge, so it does NOT need WebTransport. Still inherits QUIC's UDP reachability constraints (relay bead). Lower priority; do after native QUIC lands.
Relates: phux-84yt (native QUIC), ADR-0025 (browser web client).

subject: phux-84yt
message: Implement QUIC transport for remote satellite connections

bead.title: Implement QUIC transport for remote satellite connections
bead.description:
Add QUIC as a transport option for federation, replacing SSH-stdio for remote satellites.

- Quinn-based QUIC server/client
- 0-RTT connection establishment
- Connection migration for network changes
- NAT traversal via hole punching
- Certificate management (mTLS or token-based)
- Fallback to TCP when UDP is blocked

QUIC gives us streams, multiplexing, and connection migration for free. This makes satellite deployment resilient to network changes.
bead.notes: Mobile relationship: this is Phase 2 of the remote-consumer transport sequence (Phase 1 = wss+token, phux-5vsr). For phux-mobile, reuse quinn IN THE FFI BRIDGE (wire-identical, no Swift networking) to get connection migration (wifi<->cellular) and no HOL blocking. wss stays as the universal fallback QUIC needs for blocked-UDP networks. Browser equivalent is WebTransport (separate bead). Still needs the relay (separate bead) for NAT traversal.

subject: phux-yx6y
message: Relay/rendezvous so a phone can reach a NAT'd phux server

bead.title: Relay/rendezvous so a phone can reach a NAT'd phux server
bead.description:
The linchpin for 'connect from anywhere'. A phux server runs behind NAT/CGNAT; a remote phone (phux-mobile) cannot initiate an inbound connection to it. Solution: a relay with a public IP that BOTH the server and the client connect to OUTBOUND (NAT permits outbound), and which bridges them — like Cloudflare Tunnel / Tailscale DERP / ngrok / VS Code tunnels.

Server side (phux): the server can dial OUT to a configured relay and accept consumers routed back through it, over the wss transport (phux-5vsr). Client side: phux-mobile connects to the relay, authenticates, and is bridged to the user's server.

Notes:
- Easiest over TCP+TLS/:443 (commodity L7 proxying); this is a key reason wss is Phase 1 (see phux-5vsr).
- The hosted relay is also the natural subscription boundary for phux-mobile (local-network free, remote+push paid) — product/ops side belongs to phux-mobile.
- Alternatives to consider in the ADR: STUN/ICE hole-punching (fails on symmetric NAT -> needs TURN anyway), or requiring an overlay VPN (Tailscale) for power users.
Relates: phux-5vsr (wss auth), phux-84yt (QUIC), phux-mobile-ax5.
bead.notes: DEPRIORITIZED to P3: not needed for v1. Assumption is users Tailscale in (or LAN), so the overlay handles NAT traversal + encryption. The relay is only for users who won't run an overlay, and as a hosted convenience / paid tier for phux-mobile. Build after the auth/pairing protocol (phux-5vsr) and only if there's demand.

subject: phux-84yt
message: Implement QUIC transport for remote satellite connections

bead.title: Implement QUIC transport for remote satellite connections
bead.description:
Add QUIC as a transport option for federation, replacing SSH-stdio for remote satellites.

- Quinn-based QUIC server/client
- 0-RTT connection establishment
- Connection migration for network changes
- NAT traversal via hole punching
- Certificate management (mTLS or token-based)
- Fallback to TCP when UDP is blocked

QUIC gives us streams, multiplexing, and connection migration for free. This makes satellite deployment resilient to network changes.
bead.notes:
Mobile relationship: this is Phase 2 of the remote-consumer transport sequence (Phase 1 = wss+token, phux-5vsr). For phux-mobile, reuse quinn IN THE FFI BRIDGE (wire-identical, no Swift networking) to get connection migration (wifi<->cellular) and no HOL blocking. wss stays as the universal fallback QUIC needs for blocked-UDP networks. Browser equivalent is WebTransport (separate bead). Still needs the relay (separate bead) for NAT traversal.
Anti-sprawl note for mobile: QUIC is NOT needed for the phux-mobile-over-Tailscale path. Tailscale already provides reachability + encryption + roaming (stable tailnet IP, path migration), so QUIC's connection-migration win is redundant under the overlay, and double-encryption (WireGuard+TLS) is pure overhead. Mobile gets mosh-class resilience from Tailscale path-roaming + phux app-level state resume (ADR-0018 StateSync), no QUIC required. QUIC earns its keep only on the no-overlay/relay path (phux-yx6y) and for federation/satellites (this bead's actual driver). Treat QUIC + WebTransport (phux-0wmf) + relay (phux-yx6y) as ONE coherent 'no-overlay' bundle, built together or not at all.

subject: phux-5vsr
message: Authenticated, encrypted transport for remote consumers (no SSH tunnel)

bead.title: Authenticated, encrypted transport for remote consumers (no SSH tunnel)
bead.description:
Driver: phux-mobile (native iOS consumer) needs a phone to connect to a user's phux server directly over a network. Today that path doesn't exist securely.

Current state:
- The WebSocket transport exists (crates/phux-server/src/transport.rs: TCP + RFC 6455, one frame per binary message, PHUX_WS_ADDR) but is PLAINTEXT and UNAUTHENTICATED — fine for the local/trusted browser client, unsafe to expose to a remote phone.
- The only secure remote path today is SSH-tunneled 'phux server --stdio' (docs/operations.md 'Federation trust model'): SSH provides auth+encryption. A mobile app can't reasonably carry an SSH client UX.
- QUIC + mutual TLS is the documented v0.2+ future (ADR-0007) and is tracked as phux-84yt (P0, satellite-framed) under the federation epic phux-klxy.

Scope of THIS bead (nearer-term, mobile-driven; distinct from full federation):
- Decide an auth + encryption scheme for a remote consumer connecting to a single phux server without SSH and without the full hub-and-spoke federation. Candidates: TLS + bearer/pairing token over the existing WS transport; or mutual TLS; or pairing-code flow. Needs an ADR in phux/ADR (it closes a security design space).
- Add it to the WebSocket Transport path (PeerIdentity already exists in phux_protocol::policy) so an authenticated, encrypted remote consumer is a first-class peer.
- Confirm OutputMode::StateSync (ADR-0018) is the intended mode for a high-latency/lossy mobile link vs. Raw (currently the default); document the recommendation for remote consumers.

subject: phux-5vsr
message: Authenticated, encrypted transport for remote consumers (no SSH tunnel)

bead.title: Authenticated, encrypted transport for remote consumers (no SSH tunnel)
bead.description: phux is reachable today only locally (UDS) or via SSH-tunneled stdio. A remote, non-SSH consumer (e.g. a native/GUI consumer per the ADR-0017 consumer model, or any program over a network) needs an authenticated, encrypted path that is not an SSH tunnel. The wire codec is already transport-agnostic, so auth/encryption is a transport-layer concern. SEQUENCING: wss+TLS+token is Phase 1, chosen on reachability merit — a consumer is usually behind NAT/CGNAT, so assume the user runs an overlay (Tailscale/Headscale/WireGuard) or LAN; the overlay then provides reachability + encryption and the server just needs the token. subject: phux-0wmf
message: WebTransport (HTTP/3 over QUIC) for the browser client

bead.title: WebTransport (HTTP/3 over QUIC) for the browser client
bead.description: The browser-flavored QUIC upgrade (cf phux-84yt). Browsers cannot open raw QUIC/UDP sockets; WebTransport (over HTTP/3 = QUIC) is their only door to QUIC-class transport (multiplexed streams, no HOL blocking, connection migration, datagrams). Scope: when phux wants the QUIC experience in clients/phux-web, add a WebTransport path on the server (quinn + h3 + webtransport) sharing the same QUIC server as native QUIC. NOTE: phux-web only — a native consumer uses raw QUIC via quinn directly and does not need WebTransport. Still inherits QUIC UDP reachability constraints (relay bead). subject: phux-wurs
message: Client incremental paint leaves stale ghost cells after a coalesced redraw burst (nvim 'fucked up')

bead.title: Client incremental paint leaves stale ghost cells after a coalesced redraw burst (nvim 'fucked up')
bead.description:
REPRODUCED + SEEN (rendered the client's actual output through pyte, an independent non-libghostty emulator, to a PNG and looked at it). Symptom: scrolling/jumping in neovim (e.g. G in a long file) under the user's real config leaves STALE 'ghost' cells on the right half of rows — fragments of the previous screen (e.g. top-of-file doc-comment text) overlapping the new content. This is the user-reported 'neovim weird and fucked up'.

LOCALIZED (evidence chain, all rendered to images):
 1. Server authoritative grid (phux snapshot --cells) = CLEAN.
 2. Client output captured from a real 'phux attach' via PTY, parsed by pyte = GHOST TEXT.
 3. After a forced full redraw (terminal resize -> server resync + render_at_full) = CLEAN again.
 => The server is right, the client's libghostty MIRROR is right (a full repaint from it is clean), pyte is honest. The corruption is in the client's INCREMENTAL paint. NOT broadcast lag (server log had no Lagged warnings during the repro). NOT the SGR-fidelity bugs fixed on main today (those were color; this is erase/stale-cell). NOT a static-attach issue (a fresh attach re-synthesizes a clean snapshot, which is why round-trip tests + static captures never saw it).

MECHANISM (leading): render_at_inner's Dirty::Partial path repaints only rows whose libghostty per-row row.dirty() bit is set (crates/phux-client/src/attach/render.rs:282-366). Under the phux-jhv8 frame-coalescing (crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs:936 coalesce_defer_flags + server_frame.rs:464 defer_paint), a burst applies all vt_writes but paints ONCE on the pane's final frame via render_at (Partial). The final partial paint misses rows that actually changed across the burst — while render_at_full (paints every row) is clean. So either libghostty's row.dirty() under-reports after a scroll/big-redraw (content-dirty-per-cell vs paint-dirty-per-absolute-position mismatch: a viewport scroll moves rows whose cells didn't 'change' but whose absolute screen position now needs repainting, and the client repaints in place rather than scrolling the outer terminal), or dirty state is lost across the coalesced batch.

FIX DIRECTIONS (need hands-on debugging): (a) when libghostty reports a scroll / Dirty implies viewport shift, force render_at_full for that pane; (b) track a per-row content signature of what was last PAINTED to the outer terminal and repaint on mismatch (decouples paint-dirty from libghostty content-dirty); (c) on a coalesced burst whose accumulated change is large, fall back to a full repaint. (a)/(c) are cheapest; (b) is most correct.

REPRO HARNESS (reusable to verify a fix): /tmp/phux-diag/cap_big.py drives 'phux attach' in a PTY, opens nvim on a long file, jumps with G; /tmp/phux-diag/render_big.py renders the captured client bytes via pyte to PNG. Server-grid cross-check: /tmp/phux-diag/render_srv.py over 'snapshot --cells --json'. Needs: pip install --break-system-packages pyte pillow.

SEVERITY: high — it's the headline visible defect for the primary use case (human in neovim). Distinct bead from phux-ahk/emdv/the SGR fixes.
bead.notes: FIXED on main (commit 74b1a81), just ci green (1397 tests) + visually confirmed via pyte. ROOT CAUSE (workflow disproved the original scroll/dirty hypothesis — libghostty correctly re-dirties scrolled rows and render_at was faithful): the client resized its libghostty MIRROR to its own layout rect in the paint hot path (safe_resize in paint_focused_pane / paint_full_frame loop / the TerminalOutput arm). On the ALT SCREEN (neovim) libghostty resize() does NOT reflow, so a SIGWINCH repaint at the new smaller viewport_dims BEFORE the server's resized output arrived shrank a server-authored width-N mirror to width M<N, stranding the previous screen's content in columns >=M; render_at then painted the divergent mirror and (clipping only rows, never cols) never repainted M..N -> the right-side ghost that persisted through scrolling until a server resync. The 'forced full redraw heals it' clue was contaminated: a resize also triggers a TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT resync that overwrites the mirror. FIX: drop the per-paint safe_resize (mirror sized only by the server-authoritative TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT resync); add clip:(cols,rows) to render_at clamping BOTH axes to min(mirror,rect). Steady state clip==grid so Dirty::Partial perf is preserved. VERIFIED with an independent emulator (pyte, not libghostty): the attach-resize+G-jump repro that was ghost-ridden is now clean, as are pure-scroll and live-shrink. Tests added: render_at_clips_columns/rows_to_rect, paint_focused_pane_does_not_resize_server_authoritative_mirror (red->green).

FIX: drop the per-paint safe_resize (mirror sized only by the server-authoritative TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT resync); add clip:(cols,rows) to render_at clamping BOTH axes to min(mirror,rect). Steady state clip==grid so Dirty::Partial perf is preserved. VERIFIED with an independent emulator (pyte, not libghostty): the attach-resize+G-jump repro that was ghost-ridden is now clean, as are pure-scroll and live-shrink. Tests added: render_at_clips_columns/rows_to_rect, paint_focused_pane_does_not_resize_server_authoritative_mirror (red->green).

subject: phux-fak5
message: Graceful server upgrade: sessions survive a binary update (ADR-0032)

bead.title: Graceful server upgrade: sessions survive a binary update (ADR-0032)
bead.description:
Per ADR-0032 (Proposed). Today a binary upgrade requires 'phux kill' -> restart, which closes every PTY master and SIGHUPs the children (shells, editors, the Claude agent session) — the maintainer hit this directly (installing a fix killed the session hosting the work). tmux has the same hole; phux can close it because it ALREADY emits a replayable per-pane snapshot (vt_replay_bytes + scrollback_bytes via SnapshotSynthesizer, ADR-0013).

APPROACH (ADR-0032): graceful in-place upgrade via execve re-exec + fd inheritance + VT snapshot replay. On 'phux upgrade' the running server (1) snapshots every pane + serializes the session/window/pane tree + an fd->pane manifest into an inherited state fd; (2) clears FD_CLOEXEC on the PTY masters + the UnixListener and execve's the new binary as 'phux server --resume <fd>' (fds survive exec -> children stay alive, socket stays bound); (3) the new server adopts the fds and rebuilds each pane's Terminal by vt_write-ing its snapshot ('attach to yourself'). Clients blink-reconnect (v1); passing live client sockets for flicker-free swap is v2.

PHASES: v1 = explicit 'phux upgrade' command (the dev-iteration + agent-session win). v2 = auto-detect a newer on-disk binary after cargo install / brew upgrade (the non-dev 'just works' win) + pass client sockets for seamless. Rejected alts: kill+restart (status quo, loses sessions); separate fd-keeper daemon (cleaner crash-isolation but a permanent 2nd process + IPC seam — recorded as v2 hardening); serialize libghostty engine state (not feasible / drifts — replay the VT snapshot instead).

IMPLEMENTATION SLICES (file beads as children): (a) state-blob format (versioned — old<->new server versions straddle it) + ser/de; (b) fd manifest + CLOEXEC clearing + the --resume server mode that adopts inherited PTY masters + listener; (c) the snapshot-all-panes + quiesce (a pane mid-SPAWN / in-flight input must quiesce before snapshot); (d) the re-exec orchestration with a SAFE FALLBACK (validate the new binary via --version / dry --resume probe BEFORE committing; on failure keep serving on the old image — must never strand children); (e) client reconnect-on-swap; (f) 'phux upgrade' command + tests (incl. a child-survives-upgrade integration test: spawn a sleep/echo, upgrade, assert the same child + scrollback survived). SHARP EDGES: scrollback fidelity bounded by the snapshot (styled-scrollback still plain pending phux-q0x7); OSC8/graphics registries deferred same as attach; the state format is a maintained compatibility boundary across releases.

subject: phux-51n6
message: Latency follow-ups after the input-scheduling trio (predictive echo, dedicated input lane, state-diff mode, cheap copies)

bead.title: Latency follow-ups after the input-scheduling trio (predictive echo, dedicated input lane, state-diff mode, cheap copies)
bead.description:
The interactive-latency trio landed (commit 284fcbd): fair input scheduling (input arm before output, bounded both ways), 48KB coalesce cap + mid-burst yield, FrameAck gated on negotiated OutputMode. Verified by input_interleaves_with_a_large_pty_output_burst (red->green). This bead tracks the REMAINING ranked items from the verified latency-architecture map (workflow wbdtdk7ch), so they aren't lost:

DEEP (high effort, high value):
- PREDICTIVE ECHO ON AT THE PROMPT (#7): near-free shell-typing win — it's a flag (schema.rs ~407 default + attach.rs). Makes cooked/shell typing competitive with mosh over local UDS. Does ~nothing for full-screen app mode (Claude Code/nvim) — set expectations. Field-prove then flip default-true.
- DEDICATED INPUT LANE / MULTI-THREAD (#8): the single new_current_thread LocalSet (runtime/mod.rs ~226) is the ROOT of input/output contention. libghostty Terminal is !Send (ADR-0003/0014) so it can't move, but input route+encode could get its own runtime/thread so it preempts output broadcast. Big change; addresses contention at the root rather than per-select!.
- STATE-DIFF / FRAMERATE-CAPPED OUTPUT MODE (#9, mosh-style): forwarding raw VT (ADR-0013) re-parses+re-renders every wasteful app repaint byte-for-byte on the client. An RTT-paced server-side delta would bound the re-parse/re-render RATE under runaway output. The dormant StateSync/SnapshotSynthesizer machinery is the seed.

CHEAP HYGIENE (low felt impact, do opportunistically):
- Kill COPY #1: PTY reader buf[..n].to_vec() (spawn.rs ~232) -> reuse a BytesMut, split_to(n).freeze().
- Collapse the per-consumer pump -> Outbound mpsc -> separate writer_task hop (attach.rs ~910 + client.rs ~1078) for the single-consumer case + make write_frame take (header, Bytes body) and write_all_vectored to drop COPY #3 (encode flatten, encode.rs ~140). NOTE: the earlier 'terminal_id String clone' was a MISDIAGNOSIS — TerminalId::Local{id:u32} is an 8-byte copy; don't intern it.
- Memoize set_options_from_terminal (input/key.rs ~76) when grid modes unchanged + send Bytes not to_vec on the input encode (mod.rs ~1057).

FLOOR — do NOT chase: client mirror parse + render_at (load-bearing for ADR-0013 client-owned grid); the single IPC round trip for authoritative echo (can't predict away in app mode — mosh can't either); TLV scratch allocs (negligible). The felt win for the Claude-Code-in-phux case was the trio (anti-starvation); the rest is shell-mode polish + throughput hygiene.

subject: phux-3ns5
message: Client pane mirror frozen at attach size: resize-grow (kill/window-enlarge) leaves freed space blank

bead.title: Client pane mirror frozen at attach size: resize-grow (kill/window-enlarge) leaves freed space blank
bead.description: phux-wurs removed the paint-path mirror resize (to fix alt-screen ghost cells on shrink), leaving safe_resize ONLY in the client's TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT arm. But the server's post-resize resync (broadcast_resync_after_resize, phux-8v1) is delivered via the raw output_tx broadcast -> TERMINAL_OUTPUT, which the client applies with vt_write WITHOUT resizing the mirror. Net: the client libghostty mirror is frozen at its attach-time size and never grows on ANY resize. Shrink looks fine (render clips mirror->rect); GROW cannot fill. Repro: stacked split (C-a "), nvim in bottom, kill top (C-a k, C-a x) -> survivor stays ~19 rows tall at top, rows 20-38 blank. Server grid is correct (snapshot shows 120x39); the client mirror is the problem. Verified via pyte independent-emulator capture of the composited client frame. Fix: deliver the resize-resync as a TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT (carries cols/rows) instead of raw TERMINAL_OUTPUT, by making the actor output broadcast carry an enum {Output(Bytes) | Resync{cols,rows,bytes}}; pumps map Resync -> FrameKind::TerminalSnapshot. Client TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT arm already resizes+resets+repaints. Keeps server-authoritative mirror dims (phux-wurs principle) and fixes the GENERAL resize-grow case, not just kill.

subject: phux-l5xa
message: Agent-native composited-view introspection: snapshot --rendered (structured cells of the assembled multi-pane frame)

bead.title: Agent-native composited-view introspection: snapshot --rendered (structured cells of the assembled multi-pane frame)
bead.description: phux exposes server grid state per-pane (snapshot --cells, side-effect-free GET_SCREEN) but has NO surface that emits the CLIENT's COMPOSITED view -- the assembled multi-pane frame (layout tiling + dividers + status bar) as the human's glass actually shows it. This gap is why client-composition/render bugs (e.g. phux-3ns5 mirror-resize: server grid was correct 120x39 while the client painted a stranded ~22-row survivor) are invisible to server snapshots and force an EXTERNAL emulator (pyte) to verify the human-facing render. Proposal: a control-plane surface (e.g. phux snapshot --rendered TARGET) that drives the client's existing headless render path (RenderSink -> Vec<u8>, the attach render path already sink-injectable, phux-549) and returns the composited view as STRUCTURED CELLS (grapheme/style/cursor per cell of the assembled frame) -- NOT VT bytes (bytes would need re-parsing by an emulator, reintroducing the symmetric-blindspot/round-trip problem). Net: an agent (or a test, or an assistant debugging) can ask 'what does the assembled screen look like right now' and get cells back, with no external emulator -- closing the loop and making phux self-verifying. Squarely on the 'best terminal for agents' arc (see bd memory agent-terminal-design-signal-...). Open Qs: where composition happens for a headless query (server has grids+layout-in-metadata but layout/tiling is client-side today; either render client-side headless on demand, or teach the server to composite from grids+layout); per-pane focus/cursor semantics in the composited cell stream; relation to the structured GET_GRID idea in the design-signal memory.

subject: phux-ahk.1
message: audit: terminal_inputs per-pane log grows unbounded (write-only in production)

bead.title: audit: terminal_inputs per-pane log grows unbounded (write-only in production)
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk, verified by skeptic + maintainer read). ServerState.terminal_inputs (HashMap<TerminalId, Vec<TerminalInput>>, crates/phux-server/src/state/registry.rs:31, appended in record_terminal_input ~765) accumulates every keystroke/mouse/focus/paste recorded against a pane and is only cleared on reap (forget_terminal_bookkeeping -> remove, ~708). For a long-lived pane it grows without bound.

Aggravating: in SHIPPED code the log has NO production reader. The only reader is the #[cfg(test)] terminal_input_log_for (~880). The state/mod.rs module doc claims 'the PTY writer task reads from this log (phux-byc.4)', but that path never landed — keystroke->PTY writes happen via a different path, so the log is effectively append-only/inspection-only in production.

DECISION NEEDED (why this is a bead not a blind fix): either (a) cap it (ring-buffer / VecDeque, keep last N, or TTL-evict) preserving the multi-client merge-point semantics the doc describes, or (b) if byc.4's PTY-writer consumer is dead, feature-gate or delete the log entirely. (a) bounds memory cheaply; (b) removes dead state. Pick one with the byc.4 owner. Tests push 3 entries so any cap >= a few thousand keeps them green.

SEVERITY: medium (slow leak, only for very long-lived high-input panes).
bead.notes: FIXED on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity (unmerged), commit 4a70200. Per audit reframe (it was never WRITTEN in prod, not just unread -> no leak), DELETED the dead scaffolding: terminal_inputs field, record_terminal_input, terminal_input_log_for, the forget_terminal_bookkeeping cleanup, and the test of the dead log. Corrected the false module doc. TerminalInput type kept (live mailbox event). Maintainer decision was delete, not bound.
bead.close_reason: Done: terminal_inputs field/record_terminal_input/terminal_input_log_for all deleted from phux-server state; grep finds no production references.

subject: phux-paer
message: Split + re-attach renders blank panes (typable but no content)

bead.title: Split + re-attach renders blank panes (typable but no content)
bead.description:
Live-session repro: re-attach to a session with a split -> all panes render blank/wiped, but input still routes to the shells (can type).

Root cause (confirmed in code):
1. crates/phux-client/src/attach/server_frame.rs:279 - the TerminalSnapshot paint block is gated behind `if is_focused`. VT bytes are written to every pane's mirror (:277), but render_at (:298) only runs for the focused pane, so non-focused split panes never paint.
2. The recovery full-repaint (paint_full_frame) only fires on `layout_replaced`, which depends on the server returning a persisted layout under LAYOUT_KEY; the Attached arm seeds a single-pane bootstrap first and requests layout after (driver.rs:761-781). If layout metadata is absent/slow, the split is never reconstructed.
3. Stacked with phux-jhv8 frame-coalescing: the focused pane's snapshot paint is defer_paint-suppressed (:291) expecting a later full repaint that (per #2) never lands -> even the focused pane blanks.

Fix direction (needs care - render hot path): paint every pane's snapshot into its layout rect (the `origin` at server_frame.rs:246-255 is already computed), not just the focused one; and/or trigger an unconditional full repaint once attach snapshots land, independent of the LAYOUT_KEY metadata round-trip. Subtlety: if workspace layout isn't known yet, origin falls back to (0,0) for all panes -> they'd overlap; the fix must ensure layout is known (or defer the full repaint until it is) rather than naively ungating the per-pane render.

Found during live copilot session 2026-06-05; server was at INFO so per-pane snapshot/paint detail wasn't captured - itself motivation for the agent-observability work (self-narration + debug-on-reattach).
bead.close_reason: Done: non-focused-pane snapshot paint present in attach/server_frame.rs (~L348) with regression tests (~L1404,L1445) guarding the focused path; split+reattach renders content.

subject: phux-4r1
message: phux-it8 follow-up: reshape EOF as L1 TERMINAL_CLOSED + consumer-side detach policy

bead.title: phux-it8 follow-up: reshape EOF as L1 TERMINAL_CLOSED + consumer-side detach policy
bead.description:
Today's phux-it8 fix has the server send FrameKind::Detached when a pane's PTY EOFs. Under ADR-0015's L1 substrate, the more honest shape is:
- Server emits TERMINAL_CLOSED { terminal_id, reason, exit_status } (an L1 lifecycle event)
- The CONSUMER decides what to do:
  - A TUI with no remaining terminals in its attached Collection may choose to detach itself
  - An agent SDK consumer just notes the exit code and continues subscribing/observing
  - A recorder consumer logs the close and keeps going

Currently the server bakes the TUI's 'no terminals left -> detach' policy in. Behaviorally identical for v0.1 single-pane sessions; only matters once Collections support multi-Terminal lifecycles and non-TUI consumers exist.

Implementation when this gets picked up:
- Rename FrameKind::Detached's role: detach as a state change owned by the client request, separate from terminal lifecycle.
- Add FrameKind::TerminalClosed { terminal_id, reason, exit_status } per ADR-0015 §L1 lifecycle.
- Move the 'no panes -> detach client' policy out of server runtime; into TUI consumer code (likely crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs main_loop).
- Update phux-server/tests/eof_detach.rs to assert TerminalClosed arrives; add a separate TUI-consumer test that asserts the TUI detaches in response.

Blocked by: L1 vocabulary cascade (PaneId -> TerminalId rename; see phux-byc.5 / ADR-0015 §Doc impact / ADR-0016).

Commit reference: b5b7617 (the current shape).
bead.notes: DEFERRED 2026-05-29 (campaign triage): P3, and the ticket itself states it is behaviorally identical for v0.1 single-pane sessions — the 'consumer-owned detach policy' only matters once Collections support multi-Terminal lifecycles and non-TUI consumers exist. Moving the 'no panes -> detach' policy from server (on_terminal_exited) to the client touches the lifecycle suite (eof_detach, reconnect, server_self_exit) for zero v0.1 behavioral gain. The 'reason' field is a clean additive wire change but low value alone. Revisit alongside Collections multi-Terminal work or the q0e wiring (phux-0q8).
bead.close_reason: Done: server emits TERMINAL_CLOSED (not server-baked Detached); consumer-owned detach policy in attach/server_frame.rs L850-866 (detach only when LAST pane folds out; closing one of several keeps attach alive); regression tests L1709/1746/1775. The optional 'reason' field is deferred per CHANGELOG 0.2.0-draft.2 (exit_status is the compact subset); core consumer-decides behavior is implemented + tested.

subject: phux-emdv
message: flaky test: eof_detach::pty_eof_drives_terminal_closed_to_attached_client fails under full-parallel nextest

bead.title: flaky test: eof_detach::pty_eof_drives_terminal_closed_to_attached_client fails under full-parallel nextest
bead.description:
OBSERVED: phux-server::eof_detach pty_eof_drives_terminal_closed_to_attached_client intermittently FAILS during 'cargo nextest run --workspace' (full ~1380-test parallel run, observed twice this cycle) but PASSES reliably in isolation (5/5 across separate runs). Classic timing flake under CPU contention: the PTY EOF -> TERMINAL_CLOSED -> attached-client notification has a race/timeout that only loses when the machine is saturated by 200+ concurrent tests. On the 2-core CI runner this will intermittently red the suite.

LIKELY CAUSE: a fixed sleep/timeout or an ordering assumption between the PTY reader thread seeing EOF, the actor emitting TERMINAL_CLOSED, and the test draining the client mailbox. FIX DIRECTION: replace any fixed-duration wait with a poll-until-condition (bounded retry), or raise the timeout generously, or make the test await the specific frame rather than a deadline. Find the test in crates/phux-server/tests/ (eof_detach integration) and the EOF->close path in terminal_actor. SEVERITY: medium (CI flake, not a product bug) — but it will waste CI cycles and erode trust in green.
bead.notes:
THIRD flake observed 2026-06-07: phux::bin/phux commands::run::tests::run_nonce_is_unique_across_invocations fails under full-parallel nextest, passes 3/3 isolated. NOTE: different mechanism from the PTY-timing flakes (eof_detach, route_input_no_resize) — this is a NONCE-COLLISION: run_nonce likely derives from a coarse clock (seconds/millis) or a non-atomic counter, so two invocations within the same tick collide when the suite runs them back-to-back. Real (if minor) robustness bug, not just a test-harness issue: the run nonce should use a monotonic atomic counter or higher-resolution+random source so production 'phux run' calls in tight succession can't collide either. Worth a small dedicated fix separate from the timing-flake test-grouping.
FIXED on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity (unmerged). (1) EOF-watcher/attach TOCTOU race: gather subscribers + intern wire id + reap pane in ONE with_mut before the off-lock TERMINAL_CLOSED fanout, so no ATTACH can subscribe to an already-EOF'd pane (commit 21b5ce8). eof_detach green 3x + lifecycle suite 41/41. (2) run_nonce collision: added a process-global AtomicU64 to both phux + phux-mcp copies so two calls in one clock tick can't collide; the unique-across-invocations test is now deterministic (commit 0fb45f8).
bead.close_reason: Verified fixed: EOF ReapAndNotify single-with_mut (runtime/client.rs) + run_nonce AtomicU64 (phux + phux-mcp) are in main; eof_detach uses poll-until-condition not fixed sleep. pty_eof_drives_terminal_closed_to_attached_client PASSED in full-parallel nextest (1400/1400) this run -- the exact flake condition the bead described.

subject: phux-ahk.4
message: audit: prime_reference allocates a fresh Vec per row at attach (reuse reset_geometry buffers)

bead.title: audit: prime_reference allocates a fresh Vec per row at attach (reuse reset_geometry buffers)
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk). SnapshotSynthesizer::prime_reference allocates a fresh Vec<u8> per viewport row at consumer registration (crates/phux-server/src/grid/synthesizer.rs:803) — 24 allocations for an 80x24 grid per attach. reset_geometry (grid/reference.rs:47) already pre-sizes rows_body to empty Vecs; prime could populate those in place by swap (same pattern as synthesize_against_reference ~707) instead of allocating fresh.

Not on the hot tick path (attach-time only), so low priority. FIX SKETCH: iterate the preallocated empty row bodies and fill in place via a single scratch buffer + swap. SEVERITY: low.
bead.notes: SUPERSEDED by commit c345b49 (branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity). Rather than pool the per-row Vec allocs in prime_reference, register_consumer now SKIPS prime_reference entirely for raw (human) consumers — the reference it builds is never read on that path. So the per-row alloc cost is gone in the dominant mode without the pooling rework. The pooling optimization still applies if/when a tick-managed (StateSync) consumer attaches frequently, but is low priority.
bead.close_reason: Won't-fix (premise doesn't hold): the per-row Vec<u8> bodies prime_reference allocates ARE the consumer's reference grid — N row bodies must be materialized once and retained, so N content allocations are irreducible. reset_geometry's rows_body = vec![Vec::new(); rows] are zero-capacity empties (no heap to reuse), and prime runs ONCE per consumer attach (not per tick). The per-TICK hot path already reuses capacity via mem::swap (synthesize_against_reference). 24 one-time allocs/attach is negligible and irreducible; no real win available.

subject: phux-ahk.3
message: audit: WebSocket write_frame allocates a Vec per frame via to_vec()

bead.title: audit: WebSocket write_frame allocates a Vec per frame via to_vec()
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk). WsWriter::write_frame (crates/phux-server/src/transport.rs:216) calls frame.to_vec() to own the borrowed frame slice for Message::Binary. Every TERMINAL_OUTPUT frame to a WebSocket client (phux-web) allocates+copies the full frame, per-consumer per-tick on the WS path. The UDS path (UdsWriter::write_frame ~91) writes the borrowed slice directly with no copy.

FIX SKETCH: check whether tokio-tungstenite's Message::Binary can take Bytes/Arc<[u8]> to avoid the copy; if not, pool/reuse a frame buffer at the WsWriter level. Needs a small library-capability investigation (hence a bead). SEVERITY: low (only the WS transport; UDS unaffected).
bead.notes: Still open. Confirmed by audit (WsWriter::write_frame copies the encoded frame per send). Investigated during branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity work: tokio-tungstenite/tungstenite pinned at 0.24 in Cargo.lock, whose Message::Binary takes Vec<u8>; the Bytes-payload API landed in tungstenite 0.26. The caller also hands write_frame a &[u8] borrowed from a reused encode buffer, so a copy is needed to own it for the async send regardless. UNBLOCK: bump tungstenite to >=0.26 then switch to Message::Binary(Bytes). WS path is the browser consumer, not the UDS/TUI path.
bead.close_reason: Deferred (low value for v0.1, needs a wire-stack change to do right): the WS write_frame copy (frame.to_vec() into Message::Binary) is structural under the pinned tokio-tungstenite 0.24, whose Message::Binary owns a Vec<u8>. Avoiding it requires changing FrameWriter::write_frame from &[u8] to an owned Vec<u8>/Bytes so the WS path MOVES into Message::Binary -- a core Transport-trait signature change that only benefits the secondary browser/WS path (the primary UDS path is already zero-copy: write_all(&frame)). The clean version composes with a future tungstenite 0.26 bump (Bytes-based Message), which also lets the already-Bytes TerminalOutput payload flow through with no copy at all. Not worth the trait churn for the WS path pre-1.0.

subject: phux-q0x7
message: 9q5f follow-up: styled (SGR) scrollback in ATTACH snapshot

bead.title: 9q5f follow-up: styled (SGR) scrollback in ATTACH snapshot
bead.description: phux-9q5f ships plain-text scrollback in TERMINAL_SNAPSHOT.scrollback_bytes (history rows as text + CSI S into client scrollback). The live viewport keeps full SGR; history does not. Reproduce per-cell SGR styling for history rows too (walk Point::History GridRefs emitting emit_cell-style SGR deltas, mirroring the viewport path) so scrolled-back content matches its original colors. Bounded; additive; no wire change.
bead.notes: DEFER PLAN (assessed 2026-06-09): feasible but needs a small libghostty-rs fork addition. The history walk uses Terminal::grid_ref(Point::History) -> GridRef, which exposes style() (Style with unresolved StyleColor fg/bg) and cell() (Cell has bg_color_rgb/bg_color_palette but NO fg equivalent). The viewport path's resolved colors come from CellIteration::fg_color/bg_color (render.rs), which read RenderStateRowCellsData FFI -- NOT reachable from a history GridRef. So styled scrollback needs: (1) fork: add Cell::fg_color_rgb()/fg_color_palette() mirroring the existing bg_color_* in crates/libghostty-vt/src/screen.rs + the FFI binding; (2) phux: a styled scrollback_bytes walk that, per history cell, reads grid_ref.style() + resolves fg/bg (palette -> Terminal palette, rgb direct, default) and emits via phux_protocol::sgr::write_reset_and_sgr, mirroring emit_cell. Additive, no wire change. Plain-text scrollback already works; this only adds color to scrolled-back history (quality, not a release blocker).

subject: phux-ahk.2
message: audit: per-consumer snapshot+cursor/mode recomputed every tick (scales with consumer count)

bead.title: audit: per-consumer snapshot+cursor/mode recomputed every tick (scales with consumer count)
bead.description:
CONFIRMED (audit phux-ahk). The actor holds ONE shared SnapshotSynthesizer (terminal_actor/mod.rs:88 synth: RefCell<...>), but synthesize_against_reference is called once PER CONSUMER PER TICK (each consumer has its own ConsumerReference). Inside it, two terminal-only (consumer-independent) computations are redone N times for N consumers on a shared pane:
1. self.render_state.update(terminal) (synthesizer.rs:654) — re-snapshots the whole terminal per consumer.
2. ReferenceCursorMode::capture(&snapshot, terminal) (synthesizer.rs:718) — 5x terminal.mode() FFI calls + cursor queries (reference.rs:95-99), per consumer.
Both depend only on the shared terminal state within a tick, so with N consumers it is 5N mode-FFI calls and N full snapshots where 5 and 1 would do.

FIX SKETCH: hoist the per-tick terminal snapshot + cursor/mode capture to once per tick in tick_emit, compute the shared bits once, and pass them into each consumer's diff (synthesize_against_reference takes the precomputed snapshot/live_cm instead of recomputing). Care: RefCell borrow of synth, snapshot lifetime, and consumers attaching mid-tick. The auditor flagged only the mode FFI; the render_state.update redundancy is the bigger cost.

SEVERITY: medium (idle/steady-state CPU on multi-consumer panes; harmless for single-consumer).
bead.notes:
RISK REFINEMENT (for the implementer): the hoist is semantically identical, not just an optimization — tick_emit runs synchronously on the actor thread and the Terminal is NOT mutated during the per-consumer loop, so every consumer already observes the same terminal state this tick. Capturing render_state.update(terminal) + ReferenceCursorMode::capture ONCE before the loop and reusing across consumers cannot change output. Shape: split synthesize_against_reference(&mut self, terminal, reference) into (a) a once-per-tick snapshot+cursor/mode capture borrowing self.render_state mut, then (b) a per-consumer diff_against_reference(&self, &snapshot, &live_cm, &mut reference). Watch the borrow split (update is &mut self.render_state once; the diffs are & + &mut per-reference) and keep reset_geometry driven off the shared snapshot dims. Existing multi-consumer tick tests (tick_emit_serves_every_consumer_on_a_shared_pane, tick_emit_mixed_mode_*) guard rendering parity. Low correctness risk, moderate mechanical effort; deserves its own focused PR.
AUDIT REFRAME + partial fix on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity. KEY FINDING: the per-consumer tick/SnapshotSynthesizer path is DORMANT in production — the shipping client never negotiates OutputMode::StateSync, so tick_emit early-returns (no consumer wants state sync) and synthesize_against_reference's per-consumer recompute never runs for a human attach. So ahk.2's 5N-mode-FFI/N-snapshot cost is NOT the felt lag. The CONCRETE attach cost it/ahk.4 pointed at — register_consumer priming a full per-consumer reference + cursor/mode capture on EVERY attach — IS real and is FIXED (commit c345b49): both gated behind tick_managed, so a Raw attach is O(1) instead of O(rows*cols). The StateSync-path hoist (compute render_state.update + ReferenceCursorMode::capture once/tick) remains valid future work for WHEN StateSync ships, but is not worth doing against dead code now.
bead.close_reason: Fixed in e5f52fc: split synthesize_against_reference into prepare_tick (render grid ONCE per tick into shared tick_* buffers + 1 cursor/mode capture + precomputed epilogue/screen-toggle) and diff_consumer (per-consumer compare+emit). tick_emit renders once for N consumers instead of N times (and 1 mode-FFI batch vs 5N). Wrapper preserves the single-consumer API. Verified: 27/27 state-sync tick tests + full workspace nextest 1400 pass.

subject: phux-e3mo
message: Snapshot fidelity follow-ups: post-resync SGR continuity (#9) + epilogue mode gaps

bead.title: Snapshot fidelity follow-ups: post-resync SGR continuity (#9) + epilogue mode gaps
bead.description: Deferred from the 2026-06-08 ahk audit (lower confidence / lower value than the landed color fixes on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity). (A) #9 POST-SNAPSHOT SGR CONTINUITY: the snapshot epilogue resets SGR to 0 and the live broadcast pump then resumes raw relative PTV deltas, with no guarantee the client mirror's pen matches the server's at the snapshot boundary. Plausible narrow-window color glitch right after attach with p10k (self-heals on next prompt redraw). LOWEST-confidence survivor; the epilogue contract is SHARED with the StateSync path. DO NOT change it without a real p10k capture proving the mechanism. (B) EPILOGUE MODE GAPS: emit_epilogue restores no DECAWM(7)/DECSTBM scroll-region/DECOM origin mode, and a cursor-not-in-viewport snapshot homes the cursor to ESC[H (visible top-left flash) instead of hiding/omitting it. Both self-heal on the next PTV frame; only worth doing if already reworking emit_epilogue for (A), else document that snapshot fidelity is grid-only by design. Files: crates/phux-server/src/grid/synthesizer.rs emit_epilogue / the pump resume in runtime/attach.rs.
bead.close_reason: Resolved by documentation (the bead's accepted alternative): added a design note to emit_epilogue (e5f52fc) declaring snapshot/resync fidelity grid-content-authoritative. (A) post-snapshot SGR continuity and (B) off-viewport cursor-home both self-heal on the next live frame; correcting them means changing the epilogue contract SHARED with the state-sync path, deliberately deferred until a reproducing p10k capture proves the mechanism rather than churning a load-bearing path speculatively.

subject: phux-ahk
message: Edge-case + scroll + memory/efficiency hardening pass

bead.title: Edge-case + scroll + memory/efficiency hardening pass
bead.description: Audit and add coverage: (1) scrollback/scrolling correctness under resize, wide glyphs, huge output, alt-screen toggles; (2) memory: scrollback mirror growth bounds, per-consumer reference grid footprint, predictive-echo buffers; (3) efficiency: hot-path allocations on the tick/emit path, diff cost. Add edge-case tests (resize storms, rapid attach/detach, multi-consumer). Identify and file concrete sub-issues for any real problems found rather than fixing blindly.
bead.notes:
AUDIT PASS 2026-06-07 (multi-agent, adversarially verified): ran 3 parallel auditors (scroll/resize correctness, memory bounds, hot-path efficiency) + per-finding skeptic verification (default-to-refuted). 6 findings, all survived verification. FIXED + landed (PR #80, merged): (1) emit_instants RTT map unbounded growth for never-acking consumers -> MAX_EMIT_INSTANTS=256 oldest-evicted cap + regression test; (2) format!-alloc in scrollback SU synth -> write! inline. FILED as children for design-decision/lower-priority remediation: phux-ahk.1 (terminal_inputs per-pane log unbounded, write-only in prod), phux-ahk.2 (per-consumer snapshot+cursor/mode recomputed every tick, scales with consumer count), phux-ahk.3 (WS write_frame per-frame Vec), phux-ahk.4 (prime_reference per-row alloc at attach). Audit mandate (identify + file sub-issues) fulfilled; parent stays open until ahk.1-4 remediation lands.
AUDIT 2026-06-08 (adversarial, 17 findings all verified) — NEW high-value color-fidelity bugs found beyond the filed children, FIXED on branch hardening/snapshot-sgr-fidelity (commit 607dd22): (1) underline + overline were never emitted by the server snapshot synth OR the client pane renderer -> all neovim undercurls/cursorline + p10k underlines flattened; (2) the server's emit_sgr_delta gate keyed only on attribute flags (styles_equal ignored fg/bg), so adjacent same-attribute cells differing only in COLOR dropped the second color (multi-color ls / syntax highlighting reverted to the prior run's color on the synthesized snapshot); (3) emit_cell pushed the blank-cell space BEFORE reading style, dropping the background of colored-but-blank regions. All three surfaced only after attach/re-attach/resize (steady-state is raw byte-faithful PTV) — matching the maintainer's 'color glitch on attach'. Fixed via a shared phux-protocol::sgr encoder (single source of truth, prevents re-drift) + per-cell full-pen (Style+fg+bg) tracking + emit-before-blank reorder, with round-trip color tests. See branch for the lag work too (ahk.2/ahk.4 reframe, chunk coalescing, Bytes forward).
bead.close_reason: Hardening pass complete. Subtasks resolved: ahk.1 (terminal_inputs deleted), ahk.2 (per-tick render hoisted, e5f52fc), ahk.3 (WS frame copy deferred -- needs tungstenite 0.26 Bytes migration; UDS path already zero-copy), ahk.4 (won't-fix: prime row allocs are irreducible + once-per-attach). Edge-case/scroll/memory audit closed.

subject: phux-dacb
message: flaky test: route_input_no_resize fails under 2-core CI contention (tight 1s echo poll + transient dims read)

bead.title: flaky test: route_input_no_resize fails under 2-core CI contention (tight 1s echo poll + transient dims read)
bead.description: route_input_delivers_keys_without_resizing_the_pane intermittently FAILS on the 2-core GitHub CI runner under full-parallel nextest (passed on the 3ns5 CI run, failed on the ahk.2 run -- same code path, different run = timing flake, NOT a regression; the dims assert at L244 fails after a fast 1.2s). Cause: poll_for_echo has only a 40x25ms=1s budget; under saturation the routed key's PTY echo doesn't arrive in 1s, so it returns the last (transient) snapshot and the dims assert fires on it. FIX: poll until BOTH the echo is present AND dims==(120,40), with a generous deadline (poll-until-condition per the emdv flake-fix pattern). Local: passes consistently.

subject: phux-5pyx
message: State-sync tick path may serve stale rows after resize (same class as GET_SCREEN flake)

bead.title: State-sync tick path may serve stale rows after resize (same class as GET_SCREEN flake)
bead.description: Root cause found while fixing the route_input_no_resize CI flake: the SnapshotSynthesizer's POOLED render_state can serve stale row bodies after a TERMINAL_RESIZE interleaves with fresh-RenderState snapshot walks (attach / resize-resync). GET_SCREEN was fixed by switching to a fresh RenderState per call (low-frequency agent path). The TICK path (prepare_tick / synthesize_incremental / mark_synced) still reads through the shared pooled render_state and may have the same post-resize staleness exposure for OutputMode::StateSync consumers. No repro today (the TUI is Raw mode; tick gate skips raw-only panes). Before any real StateSync consumer ships (browser client, satellites), either prove the pooled path is resize-safe or rebuild the pooled objects on dims change. Evidence trail: /tmp phux-repro dumps from 2026-06-10, fresh-RS resync saw has_z=true while shared-RS walks read 0 chars for 5s.
